# Citrus & Salt · Tequila & Mezcal Bar in Downtown Bryan, TX

Last updated: 2026-05-03

> Citrus and Salt is a craft tequila and mezcal bar at 121 N Main St in Historic Downtown Bryan, Texas. Owned by Dustin Batson, a Marine Corps veteran voted Best Bartender in Texas. The only all-agave cocktail bar in the Bryan and College Station area. Five miles, about ten minutes from Kyle Field and the Texas A&M campus. Tuesday through Saturday, 7 pm to 2 am. Walk-ins only.

## About

- [Citrus and Salt](https://citrusandsaltbryan.com/): Ground-floor craft cocktail bar at 121 N Main St, Historic Downtown Bryan. Over one hundred tequilas and mezcals. Frozen margaritas, agave flights, full Mexican beer wall.

## Hours and Visit

- Tuesday through Saturday, 7 pm to 2 am. Closed Sunday and Monday.
- Walk-ins only. No reservations.
- Free street parking on Main after 6 pm. City lot one block east. No valet.
- Phone: (832) 285-4598. Email: citrusandsaltbryan@gmail.com.

## Owner

- Dustin Batson. Marine Corps veteran. Voted Best Bartender in Texas. Same owner as Word of Mouth Speakeasy, Concrete Rodeo, West End Elixir Company, and Vice Downtown Bryan.

## FAQ

- [News and FAQ](https://citrusandsaltbryan.com/faq/): Common questions about parking, food, dress code, private events, Aggie game weekends, and the difference between tequila and mezcal.

## Same Building

- Vice Downtown Bryan, the hookah lounge and dance floor, is upstairs at the same address. Tuesday through Saturday, 8 pm to 2 am. Same ownership, different rooms.
